reviews.review_description: Great app, makes me want to use my spin bike more, works up a great sweat, great instructors, love it

developer_response.body: We're so glad you're enjoying CycleBar!

reviews.review_description: I enjoy the app and the instructors. I love the music and I stay engaged.  I get a good sweat to start my day.  Been starting with 20 minutes so far.  I would love to get the songs used.

I feel this app deserves more praise. Around 50 workouts for one fee and it truly pushes you to the limits. I agree with some of the comments that the workouts can be a bit intense but you can go at your own pace or lower the resistance until you build up to it. Great job!

I never tried a spinning class until I got this app, and I absolutely loved it! The enthusiasm of the instructors and the song selection are fantastic. I can't believe I now look forward to exercising on my stationary bike.

I've tried other spinning apps, but this one is by far the best, and I consider myself pretty picky. The app is very well-designed, especially with the three beginner-friendly classes that gently introduce the moves. These lighter classes made spinning feel approachable and fun, helping me ease into it at my own pace.

One suggestion I have is to add special effects in the studio—this would be the cherry on top! Overall, I’m super happy with this app and highly recommend it. Please continue maintaining the energy and excitement of the trainers—it’s the key to keeping people motivated to work out. Great job!

In theory this app is great. Good layout and easy to use. However, the music is horrible and the instructors talk nonstop. Also, the music is low and the instructors voice is loud, so it’s hard to focus on the music or motivation is talking loudly the entire time. I’m debating on returning because I have the Les Mills spin classes on demand which I can watch in theater mode on the web browser. ￼

I realy like it. It is very intense for me. Couldnt Do more then those 20 minutes workouts but, looking to improve. It can only get better!
Thanks a lot for that app👌💪🥰
Only 4 because I purchased the package mainly for cycling and off course it’s the only one not working

developer_response.body:
Hello nitz72, 

Thank you for your review and interest in CycleBar! We are so sorry for the issue regarding CycleBar not working as intended. Our team identified an issue with the most recent CycleBar app release (version 1.0.101) you recently downloaded, causing the app to not respond correctly after purchase. We apologize for this inconvenience as new users were previously blocked from launching CycleBar on version 1.0.101. However, as of today, October 17, 20224 at 12:26 am EST, we have fixed the issue and new users should no longer encounter this block with the updated version. To open and use your CycleBar app, please update to the newest release, version 1.0.102.

We appreciate your review for further alerting our team of this issue so we could quickly get it resolved. If you have any other questions, concerns or comments regarding this update, please don't hesitate to reach out to our developer team by submitting a request below: 

https://support.litesport.com/hc/en-us/requests/new

We are here to help!

reviews.review_description: I love being able to use my home stationary bike while I'm IN the headset! I can't make it to cycle classes anymore, but this brings the class to me. It feels like one-on-one instructor time and helps me push through the longer workouts when normally I would just stop early/give up by myself. If you're on the fence, this is your sign to try it out now—you will enjoy it!

reviews.review_title: Not beginner friendly at all. Limited MR
reviews.review_description:
I bought the pack of 5 workouts. Finally got a chance to try this one today and can say that it is not beginner friendly at all. If you are in shape and regularly cycle, great. If you are attempting to get in shape, this is not for you.

The "Beginner Series" has you out of the gate at a 5 resistance on the bike which is not possible for someone out of shape. Then as the workout goes on, it goes up to 8 which is just insane. I have no issue with strenuous workouts at these levels, but they should not be labeled as a beginner workout since most people will be dying on a resistance of 2 if they've never worked out or haven't in years. It will be a disheartening experience.

As for the MR, I'm not sure what the point is as all you do is match a set of virtual handlebars with the ones on your bike. There is no interactivity at all. The instructor is in your room (in MR) so you can see your handlebars anyway. I guess if you are in the studio, you will see where your hands are but you can feel them on the handlebars so just not a useful feature. 

This is the second of the 5 that I have tried and they all come out of the gate way too hard. A few very basic workouts to allow out of shape folks to get their legs moving without feeling like a failure, and then slowly increasing the pace over a series of beginner rides would prepare them for the more advanced stuff. 

When I finished the first workout after being out of the saddle at 8 resistance, sprinting, changing tensions up and down, over 10 minutes my heart was pounding and it reported that I burned 39 calories which was hard to believe. You're better off getting an Apple Watch or Fitbit and using that to track your workout. This one is essentially just for company, which is nice, but there's no tracking of your  accomplishments and if you want to follow the instructor accurately, you need to be in really good shape.
